title: The 10 Best Herbal Remedies for Dry, Cracked Lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids, commonly known as lemon balm, are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the skin, reducing dryness and discomfort.
Triterpenoids, commonly known as lemon balm, are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing dryness and discomfort.
Tannins are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, cracked skin, reducing irritation and promoting healing.
Saponins are good for dry lips because they help to soothe and moisturize the skin by creating a protective barrier that locks in moisture and reduces inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a great treatment for dry lips. This is because it contains calendula extract, which is a natural ingredient that helps to soothe and heal dry, cracked skin. Calendula has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness on the lips. It also has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing. When applied to dry lips, calendula cream locks in moisture and protects the skin from the dry air. This helps to hydrate and soften the lips, making them feel smooth and comfortable. Additionally, calendula cream is gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for people with sensitive skin. Its natural ingredients also make it a good choice for those who want to avoid harsh chemicals.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal cream for dry lips, start by gathering dried Melissa officinalis leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and a carrier oil like olive or jojoba oil. First, infuse the dried Melissa officinalis leaves in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. After the infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, then slowly add the infused oil and coconut oil, stirring constantly until the mixture is smooth and well combined. Remove the bowl from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken, then transfer it to a clean glass jar and store it in a cool, dark place. When you need to use the cream, simply apply a small amount to your dry lips and gently massage it in, feeling the soothing and moisturizing effects of the Melissa officinalis.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for dry lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Aloin is good for dry lips because it has natural moisturizing and soothing properties that help to calm and hydrate dry, chapped skin.
Anthraquinones, also known as lemon balm, are good for dry lips because they help to heal and soothe irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting the growth of new, healthy skin cells.
Vitamins a, also known as lemon balm, are good for dry lips because they contain compounds that help to protect and moisturize the skin, reducing dryness and discomfort.

Polysaccharides, also known as lemon balm, are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and moisturize dry, irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy lip tissue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams are great for dry lips because they have healing properties. The gel from aloe vera plants is used to make these creams. This gel has vitamins and minerals that help to soothe and calm dry, cracked lips. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and redness. When you apply aloe barbadensis cream to your dry lips, it helps to lock in moisture and protect them from the environment. This makes your lips feel softer and more comfortable. The cream also helps to repair damaged skin, so your lips can heal quickly. As a result, aloe barbadensis creams are a popular remedy for dry, chapped lips.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams for dry lips, start by harvesting or purchasing dried Melissa officinalis flowers. You'll need a few tablespoons of the flowers for a small batch of cream. Combine the flowers with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Let the mixture steep for several hours or overnight to allow the herbs to infuse their properties into the oil. Once the oil has cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the flowers. Next, whip the infused oil with a beeswax and a natural emulsifier like shea butter or vitamin E oil to create a smooth, creamy texture. Finally, transfer the mixture to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator to thicken and set. Your Melissa officinalis herbal cream is now ready to use: simply apply it to dry, chapped lips to moisturize and soothe them.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for dry lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid, also known as lemon balm, is good for dry lips because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and protect dry, chapped skin, reducing the risk of irritation and promoting healthy healing.
Menthol is good for dry lips because it has a cooling effect that helps to soothe and moisturize dry, cracked skin.
Tannins, also known as lemon balm, have a soothing and protective effect on dry lips because they help to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation caused by dryness.
Luteolin is good for dry lips because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm dry, chapped skin, reducing irritation and promoting healing.
Menthone is good for dry lips because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from dryness and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural ingredient found in some lip creams. It helps to soothe and calm dry lips. The menthol in peppermint has a cooling effect, which reduces swelling and irritation. This makes it easier to apply a lip balm or cream and get relief from dryness. The menthol also helps to keep the skin moist and healthy by locking in moisture. As a result, dry and chapped lips heal faster and feel more comfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams for dry lips, start by infusing dried Melissa officinalis flowers in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Fill a clean glass jar with 1/2 cup of dried flowers and cover them with 1 cup of carrier oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion process, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Next, in a separate saucepan, gently heat 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter over low heat, stirring constantly until they melt and combine. Remove the saucepan from the heat and slowly add the Melissa officinalis infused oil, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens and cools. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar or tin and let it cool completely before use. This Melissa officinalis cream can be applied directly to dry lips for soothing relief and hydration.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for dry lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for dry lips because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, chapped skin and promote healing.
Saponins help to soothe and protect dry lips by creating a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Tannins (also known as Lemon Balm) are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the skin, reducing dryness and irritation.

Alkaloids, also known as lemon balm, are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm dry, chapped skin, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy skin regeneration.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale cream, also known as comfrey cream, is a natural remedy for dry lips. The cream is made from the leaves of the comfrey plant, which has been used for centuries to heal skin and mouth problems. It works by soothing and moisturizing the skin, helping to lock in moisture and reduce dryness. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to calm irritation and redness, making it a great option for chapped or cracked lips. Additionally, comfrey cream contains vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and calcium, that help to promote healthy skin cell growth. When applied regularly, comfrey cream can help to restore the natural moisture balance of the skin, leaving dry lips soft and smooth.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with dry lips, you'll need a few simple ingredients. Start by making an infused oil by mixing 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers with 2 cups of a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every few days to help the herbs infuse into the oil. After the infusion process, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean jar, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until it's smooth and melted, then remove it from the heat and let it cool slightly. Finally, pour the mixture into small containers or jars and let it cool completely, creating a nourishing and moisturizing cream that can help to soothe and protect dry lips.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for dry lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols, also known as lemon balm, are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing dryness and promoting healthy lip tissue.
Quercetin is good for dry lips because it has strong antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage and inflammation, which can cause dry and chapped lips.
Caffeic acid, commonly known as lemon balm, is good for dry lips because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, chapped skin.
Kaempferol is good for dry lips because it helps to soothe and protect the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting the formation of a natural barrier to lock in moisture.
Flavonoids are good for dry lips because they have antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect dry, chapped skin, and also have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce redness and irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea cream is a great way to soothe and protect dry lips. It contains special compounds found in the Echinacea plant, which have anti-inflammatory properties. When you apply the cream to your dry lips, these compounds help reduce swelling and redness. This makes your lips feel more comfortable and less irritated. The cream also has a moisturizing effect, locking in moisture to keep your lips hydrated and soft. This is especially helpful during the cold winter months when the air is dry and can cause lips to become dry and chapped. The cream can be applied several times a day, and some people also use it at night before bed to wake up with smoother, more comfortable lips in the morning.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with dry lips, start by infusing dried Melissa leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for about 2-3 weeks. This allows the herb's healing properties to transfer into the oil. Next, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the leaves from the infused oil. Then, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir the mixture until the wax is fully dissolved, then remove it from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a consistency similar to whipped cream. Finally, transfer the mixture to a container and let it set at room temperature, or store it in the fridge to help it set faster. Once set, your Melissa officinalis herbal cream is ready to use as a natural remedy for dry, chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for dry lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins are good for dry lips because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and protect the skin, reducing dryness and promoting healing of chapped lips.

Quercetin, also known as lemon balm, is good for dry lips because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and soothe dry, irritated skin, providing relief and hydration to dry and chapped lips.
Tannins, also known as Lemon Balm, are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect dry, chapped skin, reducing redness and discomfort.
Terpinen-4-ol helps to soothe and protect dry lips by providing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that reduce irritation and promote healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream is a popular remedy for dry lips. This cream is made from the leaves of the tea tree plant, a natural ingredient known for its soothing and moisturizing properties. When applied to dry lips, the cream helps to lock in moisture and calm irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties in the cream reduce redness and swelling, making the lips feel smoother and more comfortable. The cream also contains antioxidants that protect the lips from further damage, helping to prevent dryness and cracking. By providing long-lasting hydration and protection, Melaleuca alternifolia cream can help to heal and restore dry, chapped lips.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal cream that helps with dry lips, start by gathering a few simple ingredients. Begin by making an infusion of Melissa officinalis, commonly known as lemon balm, by steeping dried or fresh leaves in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. The ratio of herb to oil is usually 1:3, and you can let it steep for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place. Once the infusion is ready, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, and discard the solids. Next, you'll need to mix this infused oil with a base cream, such as beeswax and shea butter, which will provide the cream's texture and moisturizing properties. Melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, then add the infused oil and mix well until everything is fully incorporated. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and set, then apply it to dry lips as needed.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for dry lips

Glycyrrhizin is good for dry lips because it helps to lock in moisture and protect the skin from further dryness and irritation.
Flavonoids, also known as Lemon Balm, help to soothe and heal dry lips by reducing inflammation and protecting them from damage caused by free radicals, making them feel softer and healthier.

Tannins, also known as Lemon Balm, are good for dry lips because they have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Saponins, also known as lemon balm, are good for dry lips because they have soothing and moisturizing properties that help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from dryness and irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is good for dry lips because it has soothing properties. The cream is made from licorice root, which contains glycyrrhetic acid. This acid helps to calm and moisturize dry skin. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and irritation on dry lips. When applied, the cream creates a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and keeping it hydrated. This helps to repair and soften dry, chapped lips. The cream is also non-greasy and easy to apply, making it a convenient solution for people with dry lips. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra cream can help to keep dry lips smooth and comfortable. The cream can also be used to prevent dry lips from forming in the first place, especially during cold weather or when using harsh lip products.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with dry lips, start by gathering a few ingredients. First, you'll need Melissa officinalis flowers, which are also known as lemon balm. You can dry these flowers by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the flowers are completely dry, you can use them to make a herbal infusion by steeping them in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. The ratio of flowers to oil is usually 1:5, so for every 1 tablespoon of dried flowers, use 5 tablespoons of oil. Let the mixture sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the bottle every few days, until the oil has taken on a light yellow color and has a strong lemon balm scent. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids. Finally, mix the herbal oil with a moisturizing cream base, like beeswax and coconut oil, to create a soothing and nourishing cream that can help to hydrate and protect dry lips.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for dry lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones help to soothe and protect dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that calm irritated skin and lock in moisture.
Aloe-emodin is good for dry lips because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm and heal dry, chapped skin on the lips.
Vitamins, also known as Lemon Balm, contain bioactive compounds that are good for dry lips because they help to lock in moisture and soothe irritation, promoting healthy and smooth skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams are great for dry lips because they help to soothe and calm the skin. Aloe vera has special properties that make it perfect for dry and chapped lips. It has moisturizing ingredients that help to lock in moisture and keep your lips hydrated. When you apply aloe vera cream to your lips, it helps to calm the dryness and irritation. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling. As a result, your lips feel soft and smooth again. Aloe vera creams are also easy to use and can be applied as many times as you need throughout the day. They are also gentle and won't irritate your skin further.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with dry lips, you'll need some basic ingredients and equipment. Start by harvesting fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or purchasing dried ones, then dry them completely in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying. Next, combine 1 cup of dried Melissa leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a glass jar. Let it steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil. After the infusion process, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids. Add 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter to the infused oil, and heat the mixture gently in a double boiler until the beeswax and shea butter melt. Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool, creating a soothing and hydrating cream that can help alleviate dry, chapped lips.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for dry lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Apigenin, also known as lemon balm, is good for dry lips because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin, making it a natural remedy to lock in moisture and promote healthy lip tissue.
Quercetin is good for dry lips because it has antioxidant properties that help protect and soothe dry, chapped skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell regeneration.
Luteolin, also known as Lemon Balm, is good for dry lips because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm dry, chapped skin, reducing pain and discomfort.
Tannins are good for dry lips because they have astringent properties that help to reduce moisture loss and soothe dry, chapped skin, thereby providing relief and hydration to dry lips.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita cream is good for dry lips because it has special properties that help to soothe and calm them. The cream contains chamazulene, a natural ingredient that reduces inflammation and redness in the skin. This makes it perfect for lips that are chapped, sore, or irritated. Chamomilla recutita also contains antioxidants that protect the skin from damage and help to repair it. When you apply the cream to your dry lips, the antioxidants and chamazulene work together to calm the skin, reduce irritation, and promote healing. As a result, your lips become softer, smoother, and less prone to cracking. The cream is also non-greasy, making it easy to apply and wear throughout the day.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams that help with dry lips, start by infusing dried Melissa officinalis flowers in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Combine 1 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar, and let it sit in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process is called cold infusion, and it helps to extract the beneficial properties of the Melissa officinalis flowers into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, and discard the solids. Then, you can mix the infused oil with a moisturizing base like beeswax, shea butter, and coconut oil to create a smooth and nourishing cream. Melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler, then add the infused oil and stir well. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify, and your Melissa officinalis herbal cream is ready to use.
remedies.image_alt: Chamomilla recutita herbal creams for dry lips

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for dry lips because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect dry, chapped skin.
Luteolin, also known as lemon balm, is good for dry lips because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin.

Quercetin is good for dry lips because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm dry, chapped skin, reducing irritation and promoting healthy lip cell regeneration.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a type of plant that has been used for centuries to help soothe and heal dry, chapped lips. The cream made from this plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and protect dry lips. When applied to the skin, the cream creates a barrier that locks in moisture, preventing dry air from making your lips even drier. The antioxidants in Melissa officinalis cream also help to repair damaged skin cells, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles on the lips. Additionally, its soothing properties can help to reduce redness and irritation, leaving your lips feeling smooth and soft. By using a Melissa officinalis cream, you can help to lock in moisture and heal dry, chapped lips, leaving them feeling comfortable and healthy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal creams for dry lips, first, you need to dry the Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers. This can be done by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry, dark place for about two weeks. After the leaves are dry, you can make a tea by steeping them in hot water for 5-7 minutes. Let the tea cool down, then strain it and add it to a mixture of oil like coconut or olive oil. You can also add beeswax and shea butter to the mixture to make it thicker and more moisturizing. Next, heat the mixture gently until the beeswax and shea butter are melted, then let it cool down and thicken. Once it's thick enough, you can store it in a jar and apply it to dry lips whenever needed. Be sure to test a small patch of skin first to make sure you're not allergic to any of the ingredients.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for dry lips

intro_desc: Dry lips can be painful and uncomfortable. They can crack and bleed, making it hard to eat, talk, or even smile. But using herbal creams can help soothe and heal dry lips. One herb that's great for this is Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold. It's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help calm and protect dry skin. Another herb that's perfect for dry lips is Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera. It's got cooling and soothing properties that help reduce inflammation and lock in moisture.  When we have dry, cracked lips, it's not just a cosmetic issue - it can be a sign of a bigger problem. For example, dry lips can be a symptom of an underlying condition like eczema, allergies, or even a vitamin deficiency. If left untreated, dry lips can lead to infections and scarring. But using herbal creams with Calendula and Aloe can help fix the issue. By applying a cream that contains these herbs, you can lock in moisture, soothe irritation, and promote healing. This can improve your quality of life in many ways - you'll be able to eat and drink without discomfort, you'll feel more confident in your appearance, and you'll be able to take care of your overall health.  By using herbal creams with Calendula and Aloe, you can improve your life in many ways. You'll be able to eat and drink without discomfort, you'll feel more confident in your appearance, and you'll be able to take care of your overall health. Your dry lips will heal faster, and you'll be able to enjoy activities without worrying about your lips. You'll also be able to take care of your overall health by identifying and addressing any underlying conditions that may be causing your dry lips.
supplementary_best: To remedy dry lips, a combination of Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, and Glycyrrhiza glabra is effective. Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, helps to soothe and protect dry, cracked lips with its anti-inflammatory properties. Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera, provides hydration and accelerates the healing process with its cooling and moisturizing effects. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, adds its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to the combination, reducing redness and irritation. This combination works well because it addresses multiple aspects of dry, chapped lips: soothing and protecting (Calendula officinalis), hydrating and healing (Aloe barbadensis), and reducing inflammation (Glycyrrhiza glabra).
supplementary_avoid: If you have dry lips, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One such herb is 'Eucalyptus globulus'. This herb is known for its dehydrating properties and can further dry out your lips. Using a cream with eucalyptus can make your lips even drier and more prone to cracking. Another herb to avoid is 'Tea tree oil', which is derived from 'Melaleuca alternifolia', but also 'Zingiber officinale', as it can be too harsh on sensitive skin and cause irritation. Similarly, 'Cinchona officinalis' can be too drying and may make your lips feel even more uncomfortable. 'Capsicum annuum' or 'Capsicum frutescens' can also be too irritating and may cause stinging or burning sensations on dry lips.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera juice can help soothe dry lips because it contains vitamins and minerals that moisturize and protect the skin. When applied to dry lips, aloe vera can lock in moisture and reduce inflammation. Coconut oil is another natural remedy that can help with dry lips. It contains fatty acids that nourish and hydrate the skin, making it a great alternative to petroleum-based products. Honey is also a natural humectant that can help retain moisture in the skin, making it a popular ingredient in lip balms and lip scrubs. Glycerin, a natural moisturizer found in many foods, such as fruits and vegetables, can also be used to help lock in moisture on dry lips.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with dry lips often experience other skin issues. For example, dry skin on the hands and feet can be a problem due to similar moisture loss. Aloe vera gel can help heal these areas by locking in moisture and soothing irritation. Another common issue is eczema, an inflammatory skin condition. Calendula, a medicinal herb, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. Some people with dry lips may also develop mouth sores, which can be painful and make eating and drinking uncomfortable. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and heal mouth sores. Additionally, people with dry lips may be more prone to cold sores, caused by the herpes simplex virus. St. John's Wort has antiviral properties that can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
